events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: condensate line without trap or sloped wrong, rough-complete stage. This is non-compliant. IRC M1801 and NFPA 54 require proper condensate disposal with trap and proper slope. Decision: fail. Risk: medium (condensate can back up into furnace, cause damage, safety issues). Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective.

    "Condensate line installed without a trap, allowing flue gases/pressure to interfere with drainage and risking condensate backup into the furnace",
    "Condensate line sloped incorrectly, preventing gravity drainage away from the furnace"

  "actions": [
    "Install an approved condensate trap per OEM instructions and code",
    "Re-pipe condensate line with correct slope (minimum 1/8 in/ft per applicable code) toward the drain",
    "Verify trap depth and prime the trap before start-up",
    "Re-inspect condensate drainage under operating conditions before close-out"
  ],
  "rationale": "A condensate line without a trap and with incorrect slope does not meet IRC M1801 and NFPA 54 requirements for proper condensate disposal. Lack of a trap can allow combustion gases to escape through the drain and can cause improper drainage, leading to furnace damage or safety hazards. The work is rough-complete but non-compliant and must be corrected before close-out.",

}
